data.body:
=== RESEARCH REPORT ===
=== GitHub: LLM plan-review criteria ecosystem (Spec Kit/Kiro/rfc-reviewer/etc.)
=== agent a806d2ad536b655c8

All six research tracks completed with verbatim rubric text. I have enough to synthesize the consolidated cross-ecosystem report. No files to write — here are the findings.

---

# Plan / Spec / Design Review Dimensions Across the LLM Tooling Ecosystem

## Top-line conclusions (read these first)

1. **A dedicated "evaluate an implementation plan" rubric is rare.** Most of the ecosystem produces *plan-authoring scaffolds* (templates dictating plan structure) or *code reviewers*, not *evaluative rubrics that grade a plan as an artifact*. Genuine plan-critique rubrics are concentrated in a few sources, several of them low-star.
2. **Vendor "plan modes" are guardrails, not graders.** Cline, Roo Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Continue, and Aider all gate via tool restriction (read-only / Markdown-only) plus *human approval*. None instructs the model to critique its own plan against enumerated dimensions. Aider's architect mode is the cleanest negative case — pure model separation, zero review criteria.
3. **Rubric quality is inversely correlated with stars.** The richest enumerated criteria live in tiny/0-star repos (firdasafridi/rfc-reviewer, serbanghita/plan-critique, alaiba/arcogine). The 20k–110k-star repos are mostly persona catalogs (wshobson, VoltAgent) or authoring scaffolds (awesome-cursorrules).
4. **The strongest *structured* rubrics target code/artifacts, not plans** (AutoGen viz critic, MetaGPT code review, ChatDev, Anthropic cookbook). The plan-specific critique surfaces tend to be the vaguest.
5. **Two outliers go beyond LLM-judgment.** GitHub Spec Kit's `/analyze` + `/clarify` are the most adopted structured spec-review steps (~114k stars). AWS Kiro's "Analyze Requirements" is the only one backed by *formal SMT reasoning* rather than LLM vibes.

---

## Most directly relevant sources (purpose-built plan/spec critique)

| Source | URL | Activity | Nature of rubric |
|---|---|---|---|
| **serbanghita/claude-code-plan-critique** | github.com/serbanghita/claude-code-plan-critique | ~11★, pushed 2026-05 | **Exact-fit:** inverts plan mode — you write the plan, `/plan:critique` grades it. 9 enumerated dimensions. |
| **GitHub Spec Kit** `/analyze` + `/clarify` + `/checklist` | github.com/github/spec-kit | **~114k★**, pushed 2026-06-18 | Most-adopted structured spec review: 11-category ambiguity taxonomy, 6-pass consistency analysis w/ severity + coverage tables. |
| **AWS Kiro** "Analyze Requirements" | kiro.dev/docs/specs | AWS IDE, GA May 2026 | Only **SMT-solver-backed** validation; 5-property rubric (testable, solution-free, unambiguous, consistent, complete). |
| **firdasafridi/rfc-reviewer** | github.com/firdasafridi/rfc-reviewer | **0★** (new) | Most rigorous *weighted+gated* RFC rubric: 12 scored dimensions, score caps, per-decision "closure protocol." |
| **BMAD-METHOD** checklists | github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD | **~49k★**, pushed 2026-06-18 | Architect/PO/PM checklists (PASS/CONDITIONAL/REJECT). v6 moved toward holistic rubrics; enumerated lists survive at tag v4.43.1. |
| **Piebald-AI/claude-code-system-prompts** | github.com/Piebald-AI/claude-code-system-prompts | ~11k★, tracks each release | De-minified Claude Code plan-mode scaffolding incl. explicit "critique agent for missing steps, risks, mitigations." |

**Quotable rubrics worth lifting:**
- **serbanghita 9 dims:** Clarity (specific/unambiguous?), Completeness (all steps?), Order (dependencies sequenced?), Feasibility (executable given current codebase?), Risk (side effects/breaking changes?), Standards (CLAUDE.md compliance?), Scope (reasonable, no extras?), Testability (how verified?), Supporting materials. Plus a decomposition check.
- **Spec Kit `/analyze` 6 passes:** Duplication, Ambiguity (vague adjectives w/o measurable criteria), Underspecification, Constitution Alignment, Coverage Gaps (requirement↔task mapping), Inconsistency — each CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW, output as findings table + coverage table + metrics. **Read-only/advisory; constitution conflicts are auto-CRITICAL and block `/implement`.**
- **Kiro 5-property rubric:** Testable, Solution-free (what not how), Unambiguous (two readers formalize identically), Consistent (admits ≥1 implementation), Complete (behavior specified for any input).
- **rfc-reviewer:** 12 weighted codes incl. PRD-Traceability (bidirectional), Failure-Mode coverage (per external call), Observability ("debug it at 3am?"), Migration/Rollout (zero-downtime + rollback triggers), plus an "agent-implementable without author clarification?" challenge.

---

## Consolidated cross-ecosystem dimension list

Ranked by recurrence. **Tier A = recurs across many independent sources (high-value); Tier B = moderate; Tier C = rare/niche.**

### Tier A — recurring core (appears across most genuine rubrics)

1. **Completeness** — all necessary steps/requirements included; every component → tasks/tests. *(serbanghita, Spec Kit, Kiro, BMAD, Reflexion "missing", grocery-list, katachi, sdd-design-reviewer)*
2. **Clarity / unambiguity** — specific, no vague adjectives, two readers interpret identically; "actionable without follow-up." *(serbanghita, Spec Kit `/clarify`, Kiro, BMAD, Piebald, anti-sycophancy)* — most universal.
3. **Testability / verifiability** — measurable acceptance criteria; how is success verified end-to-end. *(serbanghita, Spec Kit, Kiro, BMAD, OneRedOak, official Claude docs, Anthropic cookbook)* — the single most consistently demanded criterion.
4. **Dependency ordering / sequencing** — inter-step dependencies correctly ordered; no forward references; "what order." *(serbanghita, Spec Kit, Piebald, BMAD, gatomia, analysis-to-plan, LangGraph plan)*
5. **Risk / failure modes + mitigations** — side effects, breaking changes, FMEA, pre-mortem. *(serbanghita, Piebald critique agent, rfc-reviewer FMC, COCO, karstenheld3, harsh-critic)*
6. **Scope discipline** — reasonable scope, no extras, "nothing outside scope changed," MVP alignment. *(serbanghita, Spec Kit, BMAD PO, anti-overengineering, official docs)*
7. **Requirement / spec traceability** — every requirement maps to a decision/task and vice versa; nothing silently dropped. *(Spec Kit coverage gaps, rfc-reviewer PRT, BMAD, PR-Agent ticket_compliance, CodeRabbit assess_linked_issues, Bito)*
8. **Feasibility** — each step executable given the current codebase; task sizing (flag oversized tasks). *(serbanghita, gatomia >3 files/>200 LOC, Cline implicit)*

### Tier B — common but not universal

9. **Alternatives considered + trade-offs explicit** — with *specific* rejection reasons; recurring worry: "straw men or genuine?" *(rfc-reviewer, COCO, grocery-list, katachi, qrspi, wshobson, Piebald, LLM-Council)*
10. **Standards / conventions / constitution compliance** — project rules, SOLID, existing patterns; deviations justified. *(serbanghita, Spec Kit constitution, davila7, BMAD, Greptile architectural drift)*
11. **Reuse of existing code/patterns** — reference existing functions/utilities rather than reinventing. *(Piebald, davila7, MetaGPT #6, Greptile, generic LLM actions)*
12. **Decomposition** — split into independent, verifiable subtasks. *(serbanghita, PR-Agent can_be_split, official subagent guidance)*
13. **Quality attributes (architecture-flavored)** — scalability (10×/100×), reliability, maintainability, observability, cost. *(wshobson, VoltAgent, qdhenry, COCO, rfc-reviewer, Greptile Layer 2)*
14. **Security / authorization** — threat model, AuthN/AuthZ at boundaries, encryption, secrets, injection. *(rfc-reviewer SAS, COCO, grocery-list, AutoGen reflection "safety", Bito)*
15. **Internal consistency / non-contradiction** — decisions don't conflict; terminology stable. *(Spec Kit, sdd-design-reviewer, katachi, qrspi)*
16. **Severity-triaged output + explicit verdict** — Blocker/High/Medium/Nit; APPROVE/REJECT/CONDITIONAL; PASS/FAIL. *Near-universal output convention.* *(OneRedOak, harsh-critic, BMAD, Spec Kit, AutoGen LGTM/LBTM, Anthropic PASS/NI/FAIL)*
17. **Context / "why"** — problem statement, what prompted it, intended outcome. *(Piebald Phase 4, COCO, katachi, sdd-design-reviewer)*

### Tier C — rare / distinctive (worth knowing, not yet standard)

18. **Rollback / reversibility** — detect/revert/verify per state-modifying step. *(karstenheld3, harsh-critic reject gate, rfc-reviewer MRP)* — rare but high-leverage.
19. **Agent-implementability** — "could an agent implement this without asking the author?" *(rfc-reviewer, BMAD's "sized for AI agents / not overly clever", gatomia)* — newer, LLM-era dimension.
20. **YAGNI / anti-gold-plating / proportionality** — no premature generalization or speculative components. *(qrspi, sdd-design-reviewer, anti-overengineering, davila7 critical-thinking)*
21. **Data integrity & consistency** — transactional boundaries, idempotency, ordering, eventual-vs-strong. *(rfc-reviewer DIC, COCO)*
22. **Migration / rollout / operability** — zero-downtime deploy, feature flags, runbooks. *(rfc-reviewer MRP, COCO)*
23. **Parallelization correctness** — accurate parallel markers, no hidden file conflicts. *(gatomia, karstenheld3)* — directly relevant to rebar's `next_batch`.
24. **Effort/cost estimation** — time-to-review or time-to-implement estimates. *(PR-Agent, Bito)*
25. **Confidence-level gating** — tiered confidence; escalate on low-confidence critical claims. *(gse-orchestrator, anti-sycophancy)*
26. **Documentation hygiene / present-tense** — record alternatives as "considered and not chosen," no "previously/replaced." *(katachi)*
27. **Reviewer context isolation** — critic sees only the plan + criteria, *not* the reasoning that produced it ("fresh eyes"). *(Claude Code critic subagent, serpro69 design-reviewer, jmolz)* — a *design technique* more than a dimension, but recurs.

---

## Critical flags (vibes vs. structured)

- **Pure vibes (no rubric):** RIPER-5 ("be specific in plans"), Cline/Roo/Continue plan modes (restriction + "detailed plan"), Aider architect (model split only), awesome-cursorrules dominant line ("plan in pseudocode, confirm, then code"), awesome-windsurf (links list), MetaGPT's design/PRD review ("review and provide feedback"), AutoGen research critic, LangGraph basic reflection ("grade this essay"), Reflexion self-reflection (free-form prose — rigor is in the *external test signal*, not a rubric), awesome-chatgpt-prompts "IT Architect."
- **Persona catalog masquerading as rubric:** wshobson `architect-review` (~37k★) and VoltAgent `architect-reviewer` (~22k★) — both list what an architect *knows*, not a checklist of questions to interrogate a doc. The two most-starred "review" agents are the least rubric-like.
- **Structured but for code, not plans:** AutoGen viz critic (6 dims, 1–10 each — strongest enumerated rubric anywhere, but for charts), MetaGPT `write_code_review` (6 numbered, LGTM/LBTM), ChatDev CodeReviewComment (6 regs), Anthropic cookbook evaluator (3 numbered, PASS/NI/FAIL), PR-Agent `/review` (rich fields but explicitly "+lines" diff-focused).
- **Genuinely structured AND plan/spec-focused:** serbanghita plan-critique, Spec Kit `/analyze`+`/clarify`+`/checklist`, Kiro Analyze Requirements (SMT), rfc-reviewer (weighted+gated), BMAD v4 checklists, COCO #226, katachi, sdd-design-reviewer, alaiba/arcogine analysis-to-plan (5-dim sweep + "zero critical/major findings" stop condition), eitatech gatomia speckit.fleet.review (7-dim, Spec-Kit-derived — closest to an emerging standard).

## Recommendation for cross-checking your dimension list

The **Tier-A eight** form the ecosystem consensus — if your rubric is missing any of *completeness, clarity, testability/verifiability, dependency ordering, risk+mitigation, scope, traceability, feasibility*, that's a likely gap. Given rebar specifically (event-sourced, parallel agents, `next_batch` file-impact, optimistic concurrency), three Tier-C dimensions punch above their ecosystem weight for you: **parallelization correctness** (#23, maps to your file-conflict scheduling), **agent-implementability** (#19, "executable by another agent without clarification" — directly your dispatch-readiness goal), and **decomposition** (#12). Also worth adopting as a *mechanism*: an explicit **verdict + severity triage with a "no critical/major findings" stop condition** (recurs everywhere, and matches your existing PASS/FAIL completion-verdict pattern), and the **reviewer context-isolation** technique (#27) so the plan critic judges the plan on its own terms.

The agent IDs from each track are available if you want me to push any source deeper (e.g. pull the full firdasafridi weighting tables, the complete BMAD v4 architect checklist, or the Spec Kit `/checklist` prohibitions verbatim).
